story.iWant: install and validate Ollama and the phi3.5:mini model
story.soThat: Story 3.1's Reactive Controller has the local LLM infrastructure ready to use
story.tasks:
- Task 1: Install Ollama Binary and Configure Service (AC: #1) - 1.1: Create installation script (scripts/install_ollama.sh) with OS detection - 1.2: Add service health check function (HTTP GET to localhost:11434/api/tags) - 1.3: Add auto-start configuration for Ollama service - 1.4: Test installation script on target OS
- Task 2: Download and Validate phi3.5:mini Model (AC: #2) - 2.1: Execute 'ollama pull phi3.5:mini' command - 2.2: Verify model loaded with 'ollama list' command - 2.3: Check disk space requirement (>=4GB available) - 2.4: Add model validation to install script
- Task 3: Implement Latency Validation Tests (AC: #3) - 3.1: Create tests/test_ollama_setup.py test file - 3.2: Implement test_ollama_service_running() - HTTP 200 check - 3.3: Implement test_phi35_model_loaded() - Model list verification - 3.4: Implement test_inference_latency() - 10 inference calls, measure p90/avg latency - 3.5: Assert p90 < 300ms and avg < 200ms - 3.6: Generate benchmark report with latency distribution
- Task 4: Implement JSON Parsing Validation (AC: #4) - 4.1: Create structured output prompt template (explicit JSON schema) - 4.2: Implement test_json_output_parsing() - 100 prompt test - 4.3: Add JSON validation and error handling logic - 4.4: Assert >95% parsing success rate - 4.5: Document common parsing errors and mitigations
- Task 5: Create Installation Documentation (AC: #5) - 5.1: Add "Ollama Setup" section to README.md - 5.2: Create docs/ollama_setup_guide.md with detailed instructions - 5.3: Document OS-specific installation steps - 5.4: Create troubleshooting guide - 5.5: Add sample Ollama API usage examples
- Task 6: Update Project Dependencies (AC: #5) - 6.1: Add ollama>=0.1.0 to requirements.txt - 6.2: Add httpx>=0.25.0 to requirements.txt - 6.3: Run pip install -r requirements.txt to verify - 6.4: Test Ollama Python client import and basic usage

criterion.title: Ollama Binary Installation
criterion.details: - Ollama installed on development environment (Linux/macOS/Windows) - Installation script created (install_ollama.sh) - Ollama service running confirmed at localhost:11434

criterion.title: phi3.5:mini Model Download
criterion.details: - Execute 'ollama pull phi3.5:mini' (1.9B parameter model) - Model load confirmed via 'ollama list' - Disk space validated (minimum 4GB available)

criterion.title: Inference Latency Validation
criterion.details: - 10 sample prompts for inference testing - 90th percentile latency < 300ms confirmed - Average latency < 200ms confirmed - Results recorded in benchmark report

criterion.title: JSON Output Parsing Test
criterion.details: - Structured output prompt testing - JSON parsing success rate validated (>95%) - Error handling logic implemented

criterion.title: Installation Documentation
criterion.details: - Ollama installation section added to README.md - Environment-specific installation guides (Linux/macOS/Windows) - Troubleshooting guide created

doc.title: Epic 3 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Ollama service at http://localhost:11434 with phi3.5:mini model (1.9B params). Python client via ollama package. Performance targets: p90 <300ms, avg ~200ms latency.

doc.title: Epic 3 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Cold start <5 seconds, warm inference ~200ms average, <300ms p90. Cache hit rate target >70%. Memory footprint <4GB RAM.

doc.title: Epic 3 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: All 5 acceptance criteria mapped to components, test approaches (unit/performance/manual), and specific validation methods.

doc.title: Epic Breakdown
doc.snippet: CRITICAL FIRST story of Epic 3. Establishes local LLM infrastructure (Ollama + phi3.5:mini) required for Story 3.1's Hybrid Reactive Controller. Estimated effort: 2 hours.

doc.title: Story 2.5 - Learnings
doc.snippet: Comprehensive testing (6/6 tests passing), HTML report generation, benchmark reporting, pytest framework usage, Loguru+OpenLit logging setup.

artifact.reason: Primary test file for Story 3.0 - will contain 4 test functions for Ollama validation
Installation script with OS detection, service health check, auto-start configuration
Detailed installation guide with OS-specific instructions and troubleshooting

package.name: ollama
package.purpose: Ollama Python client for API calls

package.name: httpx
package.purpose: Async HTTP client for Ollama calls

constraint.rule: Ollama p90 latency MUST be <300ms (critical for Story 3.1 reactive controller MODERATE decisions)
Ollama average latency SHOULD be ~200ms (optimal performance)
Cold start (model loading) MUST be <5 seconds
Memory footprint MUST be <4GB RAM during inference
ALL 4 test functions in tests/test_ollama_setup.py MUST pass (100% pass rate)
Latency test MUST run 10 inference calls and calculate p90/average
JSON parsing test MUST run 100 prompts and achieve >95% success rate
Service health check MUST verify HTTP 200 from localhost:11434/api/tags
README.md MUST include Ollama setup section with OS-specific instructions
docs/ollama_setup_guide.md MUST provide detailed installation steps for Linux/macOS/Windows
Troubleshooting guide MUST cover common installation errors and solutions
This is infrastructure-only story - NO modifications to src/ directory
NO changes to existing Epic 1-2 code (backward compatibility preserved)
Follow pytest structure established in Story 2.0 (Test Infrastructure)

interface.name: Ollama Python Client API
interface.signature: from ollama import Client client = Client(host='http://localhost:11434') response = client.generate(model='phi3.5:mini', prompt='...')
interface.usage: Story 3.1 will use this interface for reactive controller AI decisions

interface.name: Ollama Service Health Check
interface.usage: Installation script and tests verify service is running

interface.name: Ollama Model Management
interface.signature: ollama pull phi3.5:mini # Download model ollama list # List installed models
interface.usage: Installation script and tests verify model availability

tests.standards: Use pytest framework (established in Epic 1, Story 1.1). Follow Story 2.5 patterns: comprehensive testing with 100% pass rate, HTML report generation via pytest-html, benchmark reporting for performance validation. All tests must be fully automated and repeatable. Use Loguru for structured logging in tests. Generate benchmark report (latency distribution) similar to Story 2.5's evaluation report.

test.idea: test_ollama_service_running() - Execute HTTP GET to localhost:11434/api/tags, assert 200 response
test_phi35_model_loaded() - Run 'ollama list' command, parse output, assert 'phi3.5:mini' present

test_inference_latency() - Run 10 inference calls with sample prompts, measure latency, calculate p90 and average, assert p90 <300ms and avg <200ms, generate benchmark report with latency distribution
test_json_output_parsing() - Run 100 inference calls with structured output prompts, attempt JSON parsing on each response, count successes, assert success rate >95%, log common parsing errors
Manual verification - Check README.md contains Ollama setup section, verify docs/ollama_setup_guide.md exists with OS-specific instructions and troubleshooting guide
